title: Gingered Crunchy Scottish Raspberry Eton Mess
description: A plant‑based Scottish take on Eton Mess with a peppery, crunchy oat custard and fresh raspberries.
instructions: In a small saucepan, heat oat milk, coconut oil, maple syrup, ginger, salt, and pepper over medium heat until the mixture gently simmers.
Add rolled oats and oat flour to the saucepan, whisking until the mixture thickens into a silky nimono‑style custard.
Whisk in vanilla extract and lemon zest, then remove the saucepan from heat and let the custard cool to room temperature.
Line a shallow pan with parchment paper and pour the cooled oat custard into it, using a spatula to spread it evenly.
Sprinkle toasted almond slivers across the custard and press them down lightly so they adhere.
Place the pan in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to allow the custard to set and the almonds to become crunchy.
While the custard sets, gently fold fresh raspberries into the custard to preserve their bright color and tartness.
Once set, remove the custard from the fridge, break it into bite‑sized morsels, and place them into a bowl.
Top the morsels with additional raspberries, a drizzle of maple syrup, and a light dusting of fresh black pepper for a peppery finish.
Serve immediately, offering a delightful mix of crunchy almond, silky oat custard, and juicy raspberries.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
